name: Winston Lin
title: AI, software, and business systems
tagline: I'm Winston. I work across serverless, platform products, and business systems, and I also advise and invest in smaller businesses and startups. This site brings together case studies, writing, and Python tools from that work.
meta_description: Selected case studies, writing, and Python tools across serverless, platform products, business systems, and operator work.
intro_paragraphs: I’ve led product across serverless, CI/CD, and developer platforms, and I also work with operators and founders on growth, service design, and business systems.
experience_highlights: Principal Product Manager for OCI Functions, focused on developer experience and execution model
Previously led product work across OCI DevOps / CI/CD and adjacent platform systems
Built internal platform capabilities that shortened delivery cycles and reduced operational drag

intro: I’m a product leader working across AI workflows, serverless, developer platforms, and business systems. Most of my work sits in the overlap between technical judgment, workflow design, and the practical realities of getting teams to adopt something new.

Professionally, that has meant leading product across OCI Functions, CI/CD, and adjacent platform systems. Outside the core cloud work, I also advise operators, startups, and smaller businesses on growth, service design, and how to build operating models that actually hold up over time.

professional.title: Product leadership
professional.text: Lead product work where technical depth and product judgment both matter, especially in infrastructure and developer-facing systems.

professional.title: Execution clarity
professional.text: Translate ambiguous customer pain into product direction, sharper narratives, and practical execution paths.

professional.title: Public surface area
professional.text: Build public-facing artifacts too: docs, migration stories, demos, writing, and smaller tools that make a product easier to understand and trust.

personal: I like work that gets closer to the real environment, whether that means sitting with users, walking an operational workflow end to end, or understanding the business constraints around a product.

Outside of day-to-day product work, I spend time with operators and founders, follow international business closely, and stay interested in how good systems scale across different markets and contexts.
I also just like building things: small Python tools, workflow experiments, and practical assets that make complicated work feel more usable.
interests: Small tools that solve real problems
Travel, hiking, and seeing how people work in the real world
